title: Venka
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Venka.jpg]] |name=Venka |homeworld=[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]][[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] |birth=c. [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]][[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] states that Venka was twenty-seven [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] old when he was appointed to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']]. His ''Executor'' service corresponds to the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Therefore, Venka's approximate [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] date is [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]]. |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height=1.8 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]{{Film|V}} |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Sir, we have a priority signal from the Star Destroyer ''Avenger''.|Lieutenant Venka|}} '''Venka''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A [[Coruscanti/Legends|native]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Capital/Legends|throne world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Venka had a spoiled childhood, raised the son of a wealthy industrialist. Venka attended one of the oldest military training academies in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], where his many connections helped him graduate at the top of his class. After the Empire's catastrophe at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Venka was one of many non-commissioned personnel promoted to fill the void of so many [[Officer/Legends|officers]] lost in the destruction of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]]. Venka's academy connections earned him a prestigious assignment to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], the personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], at the young age of twenty-seven. By [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], Venka served as a [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] on the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]]. When Vader [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel]], the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], for incompetence during the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Admiral/Legends|admiralty]] passed to Piett, although Piett denied Venka a corresponding promotion to captain, declaring that the remainder of the [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] campaign would be a test of Venka's command abilities. Venka secretly detested Piett for the denial, but he nevertheless resolved to do his job the best he could. At the conclusion of the Hoth campaign, which ultimately saw the escape of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Vader's son, Piett finally granted Venka his promotion to captain. ==Biography== ===Privileged Coruscant upbringing=== {{Quote|The big lizard-man is Bossk, Admiral. Twelve captures, eight of them alive. That droid is IG-88, modified battle robot.''"
"''And the human with the scars?''"
"''Dengar, former gladiator. Twenty-three captures, six alive. The one in the helmet is called—''"
"''Boba Fett; I know.''"
"''They've lost count of his captures. Very few of them were alive.|Lieutenant Venka and Admiral Firmus Piett discuss the bounty hunters' arrival aboard the ''Executor''|[[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization|''The Empire Strikes Back'': The National Public Radio Dramatization]]}} Venka was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Coruscanti/Legends|native]] of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{SWE|locations|coruscant|Coruscant}} of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. He was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] circa [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], in the midst of the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]] that spawned the full-blown [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], leading to the eventual [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' Venka was raised a spoiled child, the son of a wealthy industrialist. He learned military basics at one of the oldest and most prestigious training academies in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and the many connections he enjoyed at the academy as a result of his privileged upbringing helped Venka graduate at the top of his class. The Imperial defeat at the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], in which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] succeeded in destroying the Empire's [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], created a power vacuum in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial ranks]] as well as an opportunity for career advancement. Among the many non-commissioned personnel promoted to replace the vast numbers of [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] disaster was Venka,{{CCG|set=Dagobah Limited|cardname=Lieutenant Venka}} who found himself appointed to the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], the personal [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and the premier [[Command ship/Legends|command ship]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], at the ripe age of twenty-seven [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]]. Venka worked hard for his transfer to the ''Executor'', but it was the connections from his academy [[Standard day/Legends|days]] that won him his prestigious assignment. ===A test of command ability=== {{Quote|Bounty hunters! We don't need their scum.''"
"''Yes, sir.''"
"''Those Rebels won't escape us.|Admiral Firmus Piett and Lieutenant Venka|audio=WeDontNeedTheirScum.flac|}} [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Venka served aboard the ''Executor'' as a [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] under the command of [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the raging [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] led the ''Executor'' and the remainder of Vader's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]], the [[Death Squadron/Legends|Imperial Death Squadron]], to the [[Hoth system/Legends|Hoth system]], site of the Rebel Alliance's [[Echo Base/Legends|secret base]]. At the onset of the resulting [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Vader executed [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel/Legends|Kendal Ozzel]], the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]], for tactical blundering and promoted Piett to [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] to lead the fleet, and much of the operation of the ''Executor'' subsequently fell to Venka. However, much to Venka's displeasure, Piett denied him a corresponding promotion to captain, saying that the remainder of the [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] campaign would be a test of his command abilities. Loathing Piett for the slight, Venka nevertheless vowed to carry out his responsibilities the best he could. [[File:VenkaBrandeiPiett-ESB.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Venka (left) and Piett (right) on the ''Executor''{{'s}} bridge, with the bounty hunters looming above]] The Battle of Hoth soon led Vader's fleet into the [[Hoth asteroid field/Legends|Hoth asteroid field]] in pursuit of the fleeing Rebels [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] aboard Solo's [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|starship]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. Disappointed with his crew's inability to capture the Rebel fugitives on their flight from Hoth, Vader summoned a group of six [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] aboard the ''Executor'' to bring him the elusive ''Millennium Falcon'' where the Imperials had failed. Upon the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters' arrival, Venka directed the motley group to wait for Vader on the bridge, derisively addressing them as "civilians." Piett warned Venka to watch the bounty hunters' every move, for they were all notorious killers. Together, from a distance, Venka and Piett scrutinized each of the hunters' career merits and the lethality each presented, particularly the notorious [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]. While the hunters waited for Vader, a tense atmosphere gripped the ''Executor''{{'s}} bridge. The ''Executor''{{'s}} crew felt extremely uncomfortable in the bounty hunters' presence,{{StoryCite|story=Of Possible Futures: The Tale of Zuckuss and 4-LOM|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}} especially Piett, who voiced his displeasure to Venka moments later while Venka was working closely in the bridge's port-side crewman pit with [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Brandei]], the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] operations officer.{{CCG|set=Dagobah Limited|cardname=Commander Brandei}} However, Venka temporarily relieved Piett of his concerns with news that the ''Executor'' had received a priority signal from the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] at the forefront of the fleet—Solo and the ''Millennium Falcon'' had been found. Although the ''Millennium Falcon'' proceeded to escape the ''Avenger'' and the ''Executor'' in the [[Anoat system/Legends|Anoat system]], Vader's fleet caught up with the Rebels once more above the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. With the ''Executor'' closing in on the ''Millennium Falcon'', the responsibility fell to Venka to organize a boarding party to capture the Rebels onboard, including [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Vader's son—the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord's]] true target. Arranging the boarding party proved to be Venka's last task as lieutenant, as the ''Millennium Falcon'' unexpectedly jumped into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to escape the ''Executor''{{'s}} pursuit for good. Afterward, Piett, realizing how lucky he was to avoid execution at Vader's hands for losing the Rebels, finally promoted Venka to captain. Eventually, in the aftermath of the Hoth campaign, captaincy of the ''Executor'' passed from Venka to Captain [[Kallic]], who assumed temporary command of Vader's flagship.{{DB|character|admiralpiett|Piett, Admiral}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Snap to it, soldier.|Lieutenant Venka|}} Lieutenant Venka secretly despised Firmus Piett for Piett's decision to deny Venka a promotion to captain following Piett's own promotion to admiral. Venka believed Piett was merely lucky to be the next in line to the ''Executor''{{'s}} admiralty, questioning Piett's true abilities. Still, faced with the Hoth campaign as a test of his own command abilities, Venka buckled down and strove to perform his job the best he could. Venka had light skin, [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and stood 1.8 meters tall. ==Skills and abilities== Venka possessed knowledge of [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] bureaucracy as well as [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]]. He had mechanical ability in [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] and technical proficiency in capital [[Starship/Legends|ship]] repair. ==Equipment== During the Hoth campaign, Lieutenant Venka wore the standard-issue [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]] while serving aboard the Super Star Destroyer ''Executor''. His equipment included a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. ==Behind the scenes== ===Notable appearances=== Lieutenant Venka was first mentioned in the [[1989]] first edition of the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'', written by [[Michael Stern]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The text presents Venka as an example of a standard [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] officer and provides the character with basic [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] statistics. The illustration used to represent Venka was the same image used to represent another character, Captain [[Gosden]], in the same year's first edition of the ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' sourcebook, also from West End Games.''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]]'' The [[July]] [[1996]] [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' includes a full profile for Venka with a detailed character background as part of a major expansion on the text by [[Pablo Hidalgo]]. However, ''Galaxy Guide 3'' did not explicitly identify Venka as being any character appearing in the corresponding film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']]. The [http://www.starwarsccg.org/cardlists/images/starwars/Dagobah-Dark/large/lieutenantvenka.gif "Lieutenant Venka"] card from the [[April]] [[1997]] ''[[Dagobah Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]] was the first source to identify Venka as the film's Imperial officer who informs Admiral Piett that the ''Executor'' has received a priority signal from the Star Destroyer ''Avenger'' during the search for the ''Millennium Falcon''. According to fan sites, actor [[Robin Scobey]],{{WebCite|url=http://www.aveleyman.com/ActorCredit.aspx?ActorID=37405|text=Robin Scobey|work=[http://www.aveleyman.com/Default.aspx Aveleyman.com]|archivedate=20170922221509}}{{WebCite|url=http://movie-dude.co.uk/Robin%20Scobey.htm|text=Robin Scobey|work=[http://movie-dude.co.uk/index.htm Movie Dude's Pictorial Filmography of Screen Actors]|archivedate=20170922220426}} who is credited in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' as one of five "Other Officers" in the Imperial Forces, played the Venka character. [[2010]]'s ''[[The Price of Victory]]'', an expansion of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxies Trading Card Game]]'', included an ''Avenger'' card. The card has a quote said by [[Warrant Officer/Legends|Warrant Officer]] [[M'Kae/Legends|M'Kae]],{{SWGTCG|set=The Price of Victory|cardname=Avenger}} even though the line is said by Venka in the ''Empire Strikes Back'' film. In [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]], Piett's complaint about the bounty hunters is directed at "one of Vader's captains;"[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]] Venka's rank was later established as lieutenant. The same officer appears in the [[The Empire Strikes Back: The National Public Radio Dramatization|unabridged script]] for [[The Empire Strikes Back (radio)|''The Empire Strikes Back'' radio drama]], but his scene was cut from the final version.{{ESBRadio|New Allies, New Enemies}} ===Venka–Kallic inconsistency=== While the second edition of ''Galaxy Guide 3'' explains that Venka was Piett's immediate successor to the ''Executor''{{'s}} captaincy, some sources fail to take into account Venka's involvement. The [[2002]] ''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' and the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' explain that Captain Kallic was Piett's immediate successor, with no mention of Venka.''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''{{CSWECite|II|195|Kallic, Captain}} Alternatively, ''[[The Official Star Wars Fact File 89]]'' and the [https://web.archive.org/web/20110517074252/http://www.starwars.com:80/databank/character/admiralpiett/index.html Admiral Piett character entry] in the now-defunct [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] do correctly identify Venka's relationship to Kallic in the ''Executor''{{'s}} captaincy lineage—Kallic eventually succeeded Venka.{{FactFile|89|PIE 3-6|Admiral Firmus Piett}}
